milestone: Creator authoring bug fixes -- Hosted-by-Scene image backgrounds, Change Look modal size
slice: Two real, user-reported Creator rendering bugs traced to their root in the Builder->Creator compile pipeline, plus one investigated finding. (1) A Theme Author's "Background" (a full-bleed Decoration Experience) rendered ON TOP of the Place/Holder instead of behind it -- root cause: js/services/builder.js's convergeSceneLayer() only ever sent a true Colour fill (kind:'fill') to the 'slide' compile target (behind everything); an Image/Graphics/Text Experience explicitly Hosted by Scene fell through to 'overlay' (on top of literally everything) regardless of the author's own Hosted-By choice, since js/projectModel.js's _syncUniversalContent() never carried that choice onto the mirrored Scene Layer for anything but Colour. Fixed by stamping a new hostedByScene marker onto every mirrored Layer kind (Text/Image/Graphics, not just the existing Colour fill), and having convergeSceneLayer() honour it the same way it already honours kind:'fill' -- "Hosted by Scene" now uniformly means wall-level/behind, matching what any Theme Author would expect from the name. (2) The "Change Look" mid-edit Page Style picker (reachable from Creator's Context Panel) reused the exact same full-viewport onboarding overlay (illustrated Story Meadow scene included) meant for a brand-new project's first arrival -- a quick in-context action was taking over the entire screen. Fixed with a new mode-change-look CSS variant: same overlay element/JS, a small ~560px centered modal card over a dimmed backdrop instead of a full takeover. (3) A third report -- a Builder Scene authored with zero Places still showing a Holder in Creator -- was investigated but could not be reproduced against a freshly-built zero-Place Scene (the existing holders:0 gating already correctly hides the Artwork Place card); most likely explanation is a stale, not-yet-rebuilt package on the live World, disclosed to the user for confirmation rather than guessed at further. Verified via a new 2-scenario Playwright reproduction (with-Holder z-order pixel sampling, zero-Holder gating) plus full regression across every existing Creator/Builder Playwright suite and both goldenBuild.js suites, all passing unchanged.
